page: Amazon Web Services r5b.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r5b.metal demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 81,986.24 and a Passmark CPU score of 57,096.96. Memory performance is a key strength, with read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 218.78 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 95.28 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also show strong results, with Redis achieving 77.14 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are poor, with the 7B model generating only 5.11 tokens per second.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high memory density, the r5b.metal offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access and large memory footprints without the overhead of virtualization. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it less suitable for local-storage-heavy databases or intensive deep learning training. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and caching layers like Redis.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server designed for high-memory workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database and Redis operation speeds. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is poor. This bare-metal instance provides direct hardware access, making it highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large in-memory databases, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for large in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server featuring 768 GB of RAM and 96 vCPUs optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory
Bare-metal architecture providing direct hardware access
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and enterprise apps
